effectiveQuery:
color scheme theme customization

Focus: themes
rawText:
### Create Custom Theme JSON

Source: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/themes.md

This JSON structure defines a custom theme for the pi-mono TUI. It includes a name, optional variables for reusable colors, and a comprehensive list of color tokens to customize the interface. The `$schema` field aids in editor validation and autocompletion.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/badlogic/pi-mono/main/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/theme/theme-schema.json",
  "name": "my-theme",
  "vars": {
    "primary": "#00aaff",
    "secondary": 242
  },
  "colors": {
    "accent": "primary",
    "border": "primary",
    "borderAccent": "#00ffff",
    "borderMuted": "secondary",
    "success": "#00ff00",
    "error": "#ff0000",
    "warning": "#ffff00",
    "muted": "secondary",
    "dim": 240,
    "text": "",
    "thinkingText": "secondary",
    "selectedBg": "#2d2d30",
    "userMessageBg": "#2d2d30",
    "userMessageText": "",
    "customMessageBg": "#2d2d30",
    "customMessageText": "",
    "customMessageLabel": "primary",
    "toolPendingBg": "#1e1e2e",
    "toolSuccessBg": "#1e2e1e",
    "toolErrorBg": "#2e1e1e",
    "toolTitle": "primary",
    "toolOutput": "",
    "mdHeading": "#ffaa00",
    "mdLink": "primary",
    "mdLinkUrl": "secondary",
    "mdCode": "#00ffff",
    "mdCodeBlock": "",
    "mdCodeBlockBorder": "secondary",
    "mdQuote": "secondary",
    "mdQuoteBorder": "secondary",
    "mdHr": "secondary",
    "mdListBullet": "#00ffff",
    "toolDiffAdded": "#00ff00",
    "toolDiffRemoved": "#ff0000",
    "toolDiffContext": "secondary",
    "syntaxComment": "secondary",
    "syntaxKeyword": "primary",
    "syntaxFunction": "#00aaff",
    "syntaxVariable": "#ffaa00",
    "syntaxString": "#00ff00",
    "syntaxNumber": "#ff00ff",
    "syntaxType": "#00aaff",
    "syntaxOperator": "primary",
    "syntaxPunctuation": "secondary",
    "thinkingOff": "secondary",
    "thinkingMinimal": "primary",
    "thinkingLow": "#00aaff",
    "thinkingMedium": "#00ffff",
    "thinkingHigh": "#ff00ff",
    "thinkingXhigh": "#ff0000",
    "bashMode": "#ffaa00"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Theme Format Example

Source: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/themes.md

This JSON example illustrates the fundamental structure of a pi-mono theme file. It shows the required `name` field, an optional `vars` section for defining reusable color values, and the `colors` object where all UI elements are assigned specific colors or references to variables.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/badlogic/pi-mono/main/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/theme/theme-schema.json",
  "name": "my-theme",
  "vars": {
    "blue": "#0066cc",
    "gray": 242
  },
  "colors": {
    "accent": "blue",
    "muted": "gray",
    "text": "",
    ...
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Select Theme via Settings JSON

Source: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/themes.md

This JSON snippet demonstrates how to select a theme by specifying its name in the `settings.json` file. This is a common method for persistent theme configuration.

```json
{
  "theme": "my-theme"
}
```

### Themes > Creating a Custom Theme

Source: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/themes.md

Creating a custom theme involves defining a JSON file that specifies all the necessary color tokens. First, create a theme file, for example, `~/.pi/agent/themes/my-theme.json`. This file should include a `name`, optional `vars` for reusable color definitions, and a `colors` object containing all 51 required color tokens. The `$schema` field is recommended for enabling editor auto-completion and validation. After defining your theme, you can select it via `/settings`. Pi offers hot-reloading for custom themes, meaning any edits to the active custom theme file will be automatically reloaded for immediate visual feedback.

--------------------------------

### Themes > Theme Format

Source: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/themes.md

The theme format is a JSON object that includes a required `name` for unique identification, an optional `vars` object for defining reusable color values, and a mandatory `colors` object that must define all 51 required color tokens. The `$schema` field at the root of the JSON file is highly recommended as it enables editor auto-completion and validation, ensuring your theme adheres to the expected structure. Within the `vars` object, you can define custom color names (e.g., `blue`, `gray`) and assign them hexadecimal color codes or ANSI color codes. These defined variables can then be referenced by name within the `colors` object.
